hero.name: Phuong Bui Thanh
hero.lede: Senior Frontend Developer with 8 years of experience in JavaScript/TypeScript, building, testing and operating high-performance web applications at enterprise scale. Deep expertise in **React/Next.js** with **Server-Side Rendering (SSR)**, bundle optimization, Micro Frontend architecture (Nx Monorepo), and CI/CD for containerized deployments (Docker, Kubernetes). Strong TypeScript foundation across both the React/Next.js and **Angular** ecosystems — and a hands-on team leader who led a frontend team of 8 in a 60-person logistics program.

platformMap.title: VELA · the frontend I architected
platformMap.note: Each portal gets the architecture its delivery needs — a single-application Angular Core Portal owned end-to-end by one team; Customer and Supplier ship as independent Angular Nx Micro Frontend platforms so teams deploy in parallel.

rows.name: VELA Logistics Platform
rows.meta: Indo Trans Logistics · Frontend Team Leader · Jul 2023 – Mar 2026
rows.problem: A multi-tenant logistics platform for internal and external users — three portals, one 60-person program, teams that needed to ship in parallel without stepping on each other.
rows.decision: Three repositories, all multi-tenant: a **single-application Angular Core Portal**, and **separate Angular Nx Monorepo Micro Frontend platforms** (host + remotes) for the Customer and Supplier portals. Jasmine + SonarQube quality gates in a Docker/Kubernetes CI/CD pipeline.
rows.outcome: Independent deployments and parallel team development; Core Portal initial bundle cut from **12 MB to 3 MB** (webpack-bundle-analyzer); led the team of 8 that built and shipped it.
rows.role: Architecture, engineering standards, code review workflow, GitFlow strategy — while staying hands-on across the Angular stack, including the Core Portal performance work.
rows.arch:
**vela frontend** · 3 repos · all multi-tenant
├── core-portal        **Angular · single app**          — internal operations
├── customer-portal    **Angular · Nx monorepo · MFE**  — host + remotes
└── supplier-portal    **Angular · Nx monorepo · MFE**  — host + remotes
rows.tech: Angular · TypeScript · RxJS · Nx · Module Federation · Docker · Kubernetes · .NET · Jasmine · SonarQube

rows.name: SOGUS — Examination Platform
rows.problem: Candidate screening with multiple assessment formats — and two very different audiences: internal administrators and candidates taking tests.
rows.decision: Led frontend design across two applications — **React/Next.js (Pages Router)** with **Server-Side Rendering (SSR)** for the candidate-facing landing, interview and testing flows (SEO-critical pages, fast first render), and **Angular** for the internal administration portal.
rows.outcome: A unified exam lifecycle across both apps, delivered by a 10-person project team with the frontend workstream led by me.
rows.role: Frontend architecture across both stacks, task planning, PR review, stakeholder communication.
rows.tech: Angular · React · Next.js · SSR · TypeScript · Java · PostgreSQL

rows.name: Medbuy — Pharmaceutical E-commerce
rows.problem: E-commerce connecting pharmaceutical suppliers, agents and end users — responsive UX with reliable backend integration.
rows.decision: Developed responsive, design-accurate UIs in **React/Next.js**, applying **SSR** for product and listing pages; integrated REST APIs with the **NestJS** backend; containerized deployment with **Docker**.
rows.outcome: Design-accurate, responsive interfaces shipped to production with containerized deployment.
rows.role: Frontend implementation and API integration on a 10-person project team.
rows.tech: React · Next.js · SSR · NestJS · TypeScript · Docker

rows.name: Kensa Order System
rows.problem: Order and revenue management with Admin and Customer portals, including live payment.
rows.decision: End-to-end build in a two-person team: React frontend, Laravel REST APIs, VNPay and Payoo payment integration, Docker deployment on Linux.
rows.outcome: Order processing, reporting and live payment flows running in production.
rows.role: Everything frontend-to-backend — the project that taught me what full ownership costs.

items.org: Indo Trans Logistics — Ho Chi Minh City
items.desc: Architected the Angular frontend for all 3 portals of Indo Trans Logistics' VELA platform — a single-application Core Portal and an Nx-based Micro Frontend platform (1 host + multiple remotes) for the multi-tenant Customer and Supplier Portals. Cut the Core Portal's initial bundle from 12 MB to 3 MB through bundle analysis, code splitting and route-based lazy loading. Built Docker/Kubernetes CI/CD across 4 repositories with Jasmine unit tests and SonarQube quality gates, and refactored legacy UI modules (Bootstrap → Tailwind) while keeping production releases stable. Led the team of 8 frontend developers — hands-on throughout — in a 60-person program.

items.org: AMIT Group Joint Stock Company — Ho Chi Minh City
items.desc: Developed and delivered enterprise and client-facing web applications with React/Next.js in TypeScript, using Server-Side Rendering (SSR) for SEO-critical, data-heavy views (SOGUS, Medbuy). Built and integrated REST APIs with Node.js (NestJS) and Laravel, and deployed on Linux with Docker. Led small frontend teams (3–5 developers) across concurrent client projects, with estimation, technical scoping and direct customer communication alongside delivery.

items.org: AMIT Group Joint Stock Company — Ho Chi Minh City
items.desc: Built responsive web applications with React, Angular and WordPress for enterprise clients; developed fullstack features with PHP/Laravel and MySQL (Kensa Order System).

items.org: AMIT Group — Ho Chi Minh City
items.desc: PSD-to-HTML/CSS implementation and AJAX-driven interactions, supporting senior developers before returning full-time after graduation.
